payload.title: Familiarize Narada with Plural Embodiment, Singular Authority
payload.summary: The thoughts repository now contains a new concept document, Plural Embodiment, Singular Authority, naming the discipline of allowing many optimized system embodiments while routing governed mutation through one declared authority locus. The concept arose from Windows/WSL Narada invocation and clone-authority friction and appears directly relevant to Narada proper authority routing, doctor/bootstrap ergonomics, inbox submission, task mutation safety, and architect CLI resume/hydration.
evidence: Thoughts commit pushed: 3bd5434 Add plural embodiment singular authority concept.
Core rule: many embodiments may present, inspect, or assist; one declared authority locus must own each governed mutation.
proposal: Narada proper should familiarize itself with this concept and evaluate whether it should be admitted into Narada knowledge/doctrine through the appropriate charter or governed knowledge-admission path, if such a charter exists.

If admitted, use the concept to guide authority-clone routing, cross-environment CLI wrappers, narada doctor reporting, inbox submission preflight, task mutation locus guards, and narada resume --with <architect-tool> semantics.
payload.recommendation: Route to the relevant Narada knowledge/doctrine charter if present; otherwise record as a pending knowledge-admission proposal rather than immediately converting it into an implementation task.

promotion.note: Envelope archived; no target-zone mutation was performed.
